canonical: ACLS (Automatic Carrier Landing System) Tutorial / Case III Recovery (Supercarrier)
alternates: ACLS (Automatic Carrier Landing System) Tutorial / Case III Recovery (Supercarrier)

source.section: Part 15 - Navigation & Case III Recovery > 10 - ACLS (Automatic Carrier Landing System) Tutorial / Case III Recovery (Supercarrier)

steps.action: Contact Carrier to turn on the lights Left click on the COMM1 knob to pull it and select COMM1 Radio Scroll mousewheel on COMM1 Radio Channel Selector to M (Manual) Mode Press the OSB next to AM or FM to select the FM frequency (“:FM” will appear when selected) Press CLR on the UFC to clear current frequency Type “127500” on the UFC to set carrier radio frequency 127.5 MHz Press ENT on the UFC to enter this frequency Press the COMM switch – COMM1 on your throttle to contact the carrier (RALT+\) Go in F5 – AT5 menu, then to the CVN-73 (George Washington Carrier) menu, then to the F1 – Inbound menu. And that’s it, the carrier is now illuminated. The carrier will then give you a final bearing (353 in this tutorial).
Adjust your interior cockpit lights as required
Set your HSI page on the left DDI, your FCS page on the right DDI, and the CHKLST page on the center AMPCD.

Set ALTITUDE Switch to RDR to use your radar altimeter as a reference for your HUD and set radar altimeter index to 370 ft or 320 (as you prefer). You use 370 ft to remind you that you need to make the ball call or 320 ft to make sure you have the proper altitude when 3/4 nm from the carrier. 10 – ACLS (AUTOMATIC CARRIER LANDING SYSTEM) TUTORIAL CASE III RECOVERY (SUPERCARRIER) (Note: we will assume the carrier’s TACAN frequency is 73X, our ICLS channel is 11 and our Link 4 Datalink Frequency is 336.0 MHz.)
On the UFC (Up-Front Control) Panel, press the TCN button and press the ON/OFF button if the ON indication is extinguished.
Press the T/R OSB button to set it to Transmit/Receive. The “:” symbol indicates that it is selected.
Press “73” on the scratchpad and press “ENT” to enter frequency.
Press the X or Y OSB to select the right letter of the TACAN frequency (73X in our case).
On the UFC (Up-Front Control) Panel, press the ILS button and press the ON/OFF button if the ON indication is extinguished.
Press “11” on the scratchpad and press “ENT” to enter ICLS channel.
On the UFC (Up-Front Control) Panel, press the D/L (Datalink) button once to access the Link 4 Datalink menu, and then press the ON/OFF button if the ON indication is extinguished.
Press “3360” on the scratchpad and press “ENT” to enter Link 4 Datalink frequency (336.0 MHz).
On the left DDI, which should be set to the HSI (Horizontal Situation Indicator) page, press OSB next to ACL (Automatic Carrier Landing).

For a few seconds, the Automatic Carrier System will perform a handshake test with the carrier using the Link 4 Datalink frequency (336.0 MHz) we entered previously. For the duration of the test, “TEST” indication is visible. “UTM FAIL” indication means the Link 4 datalink frequency Is not set correctly.
Once test is finished and datalink handshake process is performed, the “TEST” indication will disappear and the “ACL 1” indication will be visible.
On right DDI, set the SUPT HSI (Horizontal Situation Display) page.
If not selected/boxed already, press the OSB next to ILS to select tracking mode to ICLS. Once selected, ILS should be boxed.
Press the OSB next to TACAN to select tracking mode to TACAN. Once selected, TCN should be boxed.
Click and hold (Left/Right mouse button) the CRS (Course Set) knob to set desired course (CSEL) for TACAN approach (353 for the Marshal Radial and Final Bearing).
When you are 25 nm from carrier, follow the Marshal Radial (353) and descend from 10,000 ft to 6,000 ft at 300 kts with a descent rate of 4000 ft/min.
Once you are on the Marshal Radial (353) at 6000 ft and 300 kts, line up the aircraft the Final Bearing radial (353), then call “Established” on the radio.
The “DATA” indication on the HUD means that you have received new commands from the carrier via the Link 4 Datalink.

On the ACLS page, you will notice the following instructions being sent to you to perform the Case III Recovery: CMD A/S (Commanded Airspeed): 300 kts CMD ALT (Commanded Altitude): 6000 ft CMD ROD (Commanded Rate of Descent): 0 ft/min since we are already at 6000 ft T/C: Traffic & Control Mode 10 – ACLS (AUTOMATIC CARRIER LANDING SYSTEM) TUTORIAL CASE III RECOVERY (SUPERCARRIER) Distance to Carrier (nm) Altitude (ft) Airspeed (kts) Bearing/Distance to Carrier (nm)
Once you are 20 nm from the Carrier, call “Commencing” on the radio and descend to 5000 ft at 250 kts with a descent rate of 4000 ft/min.
The “DATA” indication on the HUD means that you have received new commands from the carrier via the Link 4 Datalink.

On the ACLS page, you will notice the following instructions being sent to you to perform the Case III Recovery: CMD A/S (Commanded Airspeed): 250 kts CMD ALT (Commanded Altitude): 5000 ft CMD ROD (Commanded Rate of Descent): 4000 ft/min T/C: Traffic & Control Mode The Double Chevrons indicate the position of the carrier.
Perform a “Check In” call for the approach on the radio and confirm the final bearing (353).
When at 5000 ft, call “Platform” on the radio and descend to 1200 ft at 250 kts with a descent rate of 2000 ft/min.
The “DATA” indication on the HUD means that you have received new commands from the carrier via the Link 4 Datalink.

On the ACLS page, you will notice the following instructions being sent to you to perform the Case III Recovery: CMD A/S (Commanded Airspeed): 250 kts CMD ALT (Commanded Altitude): 1200 ft CMD ROD (Commanded Rate of Descent): 2000 ft/min T/C: Traffic & Control Mode The Double Chevrons indicate the position of the carrier.
When reaching 10 nm from carrier, set landing gear down, full flaps, On Speed AoA, Anti-Skid Switch to OFF, Hook Lever DOWN, Hook Bypass Switch to CARRIER.
When reaching 8 nm from carrier, reduce speed to 140 kts (with the throttle) and maintain 1200 ft.
The “DATA” indication on the HUD means that you have received new commands from the carrier via the Link 4 Datalink.

On the ACLS page, you will notice the following instructions being sent to you to perform the Case III Recovery: CMD A/S (Commanded Airspeed): 140 kts CMD ALT (Commanded Altitude): 1200 ft CMD ROD (Commanded Rate of Descent): 2000 ft/min T/C: Traffic & Control Mode LND CHK indication: aircraft should be in landing configuration (gear down, hook down, flaps down, etc.) and landing checks should be performed. The Double Chevrons indicate the position of the carrier.
When entering final bearing, the localizer line is used to guide yourself laterally on the runway. Line up Velocity Vector with the Localizer Line. « Chase » the line.

When reaching approx. 7 nm from the carrier, the glide slope line will appear. The glide slope line is used to guide yourself vertically on the glide path. Line up Velocity Vector with the Glide Slope Line. « Chase » the line.

When reaching 6 nm from carrier and flying at 1200 ft altitude and 140 kts airspeed, the carrier will call “ACLS Lock On” followed by your current distance and a “Say Needles” call. The ACLS page will display ACL RDY MODE 1.
Engage ACLS Mode 1 to perform a fully automatic landing. Press “AP” button, then select “CPL” (Coupled) Autopilot mode.

The HUD will display “CPLD P/R” (Coupled Autopilot Pitch/Roll) and ATC (Automatic Throttle Controller). The throttle will maintain 140 kts while the autopilot will ensure the aircraft is on a correct localizer and glide slope to land on the aircraft carrier. The “Tadpole” symbol represents the ideal glide slope determined by the datalink system.
At this point, the aircraft should land on the carrier by itself.
When at 3/4 nm, call the ball with the radio.
Note: The TILT message appears on the HUD if you lose datalink for more than two seconds.
